summary: EXACT-parameter figure reproduction of arXiv:2308.10361 Sec. V.A in AthenaK Z4c-CCM: r_c=20, tau=2, Cauchy boundary (worldtube analog) 41, psi4 extraction r=36, X in {1e-5, 2}; h=1/2 (164^3) with 132^3/104^3 error-estimate companions and a causally disconnected 416^3 reference (boundary 104; the 124-box variant measured 0.90 s/cycle at 496^3 and breached the 10-min cap; 104 is still exactly disconnected: contamination reaching r=36 by t=80 is bounded by e^-1024 X in float64). X=1e-5 on 4 GPUs: E(somm)=E(ccm)=2.732e-4, gap 0.0% (indistinguishability, the paper's perturbative conclusion) and BOTH << the two-resolution numerical-error estimate 0.70 (the paper's bottom-panel hierarchy: boundary error below truncation error). X=2: stable to t=80 with perturbative ID under kappa-damping, E(somm)=E(ccm)=8.87e-2, constraints bounded then damped after t~35. CCM-Sommerfeld waveform difference is nonzero exactly after boundary->extraction causal contact (t>=64.5) at rel 5e-10..4e-9: the r^-5 peeling suppression of the outgoing self-datum psi0 at a r=41 worldtube; the X=2 figure-level CCM-vs-CCE distinction therefore requires the CCE-coupled datum (open). Scri-formula amplitude deficit RESOLVED as FD truncation, not extraction radius: peak ratio climbs 0.292/0.465/0.612 at 104/132/164^3 (~4 grid points per tau, 2nd-order stencils)

summary: REFERENCE = ANALYTIC (mission-3 stage 0, OVERALL PASS twice over). (a) Exact linear finite-radius waveform derived and 7-gate verified (verify_n12_exact_psi4.py): in the AthenaK extraction convention rpsi4(2,0) = sqrt(6pi/5)[F6 + 2F5/r + 3F4/r^2 + 3F3/r^3 + (3/2)F2/r^4](t-r) - sqrt(6pi/5)(3/2)F2(t+r)/r^4; gates: traceless, linearized-vacuum coefficient-exact, sin^2-factorization at 4 Pythagorean angles, peeling k+j=6, scri limit (convention map rpsi4_AthenaK = -1 x paper formula), psi0 datum channel EXACT (-sqrt(27pi/10)F2(t-r)/r^5*2Y20, no other retarded term; orientation map: datum channel = (E - eps(s)B)(m,m) in the concrete right-handed frame, = N3's (E+epsB) under eps->-eps), independent mpmath-FD cross-check 1.4e-40. (b) AthenaK 6th-order ladder (nghost=4; 164/248/296^3, box 41, window t in [40,58]) converges to the exact waveform: E = 4.99e-2/5.36e-3/2.02e-3, measured order 5.39/5.52, finest-rung amplitude 1 +- 2.0e-4 and time shift 0.000 — the reference IS the analytic solution to quantified 6th-order truncation. TWO BUGS root-caused en route (error-db): pgen frame eth = -theta-hat flipping h_{r theta} (linearized-vacuum violation at 5% of |Ricci|, invisible to constraint monitors and run-vs-ref protocols); waveform time label one full step behind the Task_End state. Checker lesson encoded: rungs compared at their OWN sample times (cross-rung linear interpolation over the snapped cadence dominates the error budget)

summary: N14 stage A1 ADMITTED (OVERALL PASS): the linear characteristic psi0 chain closes exactly against the N12 datum. Bondi-like J from the corrected Teukolsky solution = angular metric part (3/4)s^2[F4/r+2F3/r^2+3F2/r^3+3F1/r^4+3F0/r^5] PLUS the g_{r theta}-removing angular gauge shift (xi^th from -h_rth/r^2, xi(scri)=0): the shift cancels the F3/r^2 term EXACTLY (C1), restoring peeling. Linearized eq:psi0cce (psi0 = -J'/(2r) - J''/4 at fixed u) on J_full gives (3/4)s^2[(3/2)F2/r^5 + 9F1/r^6 + 9F0/r^7]: leading coefficient 9/8 = sqrt(27pi/10)*(1/4)sqrt(15/2pi) EXACTLY (C2, sympy), no term below r^-5 (C4), numeric closure 4.4e-34 (C3, mpmath FD). Convention map: eq:psi0cce frame = (-1) x datum at leading peeling order. STAGE-B CONSEQUENCE (load-bearing): the live worldtube J requires h_thth, h_phph AND the radial integral of h_{r theta}/r^2 over the characteristic domain (the ifc worldtube-transformation chain) — angular metric alone produces a peeling-violating F3/r^4 psi0 term. Remaining stage A: radial hierarchy solver (beta/Q/U/W/H) + evolution gate; W-equation Minkowski-background puzzle still open (G0, design doc)

summary: N14 stage C part 2 (iter 44): GENUINELY LIVE CCM - the full chain Cauchy ADM -> 3-sphere l=2 m=0 worldtube sampling -> six-row map (+ beta) -> anchored-gauge BCs -> native in-process characteristic solver -> lagged-cone psi0 probe -> mode-5 datum runs end-to-end on 4 GPUs, stable to t=80. Three structural advances, each ZccmJl-verified before the C++ port: (1) GAUGE: the live (anchored) gauge has beta != 0 and non-decaying tails; the ringed scheme fails on it at O(10^3-10^4) in psi0 (documented-failure pin); machine-derived beta-corrected hierarchy (exact-rational joint fits: Q source -4 r eth(beta), W source -(4/3) ethb eth beta, H eq jointly refit) + UNRINGED t-scalar solver: machine-exact sweeps both gauges, anchored end-to-end psi0 probe rel 8.8e-5 (7.1e-6 at du/2); psi0 J-only formula gauge-invariant exactly. (2) TIMING: cone label = retarded time u = t - r (pre-fix mode 4 mistimed the datum by rwt under the 7.6e-9 gate); boundary datum = interior probe at r_B on cone u = t - r_B with history-deque lag; interior probe BETTER conditioned than the tube (8.8e-5 vs 6e-2); phase-fixed mode4 vs mode3 = 4.1e-9 of peak. (3) HONEST CONDITIONING FINDING: sampled tube data is faithful (hTT err 5.4e-2 of peak = the admitted h=0.5 Cauchy truncation) but psi0_true/J ~ 1e-8 (peeling) means datum FIDELITY needs ~1e-11-relative tube data - unreachable by refinement; on the purely outgoing X=1e-5 test the live datum is truncation junk at 5.7e5x the (essentially zero) true datum with waveform imprint 5.7e-5 of peak = 880x BELOW waveform truncation. Live CCM: stable + harmless PASS; datum fidelity NOT demonstrable on this test by physics - discharge plan: characteristic-pulse-injection test (Sec V.C) where psi0 is O(1) of the data

summary: N14 datum-FIDELITY (iter 45): the 2308.10361 Sec V.C characteristic-pulse-injection test PASSES through the LIVE native solver (mode 6: ingoing l=2 m=0 J-pulse in the characteristic initial data, paper shape/parameters, over quiescent Cauchy ID; conditioning psi0/J = 7.0e-3 vs 1e-8 on the outgoing test - the designed fidelity regime). ZccmJl first: pulse-ID testset 8/8 (normalization exact rtol 1e-12; radial ladder n49/n65/n97: n=65 converged to 2.1e-7 of peak; du error 4.3e-12; mirror quiescent-BC reference series admitted, n14_pulse_ref.csv). C++ standalone port gate: trough/peak rel 1.3e-7/2.3e-7 vs the reference (production advance->probe_query pattern). Live GPU battery (t=180, 4 GPUs, stable): Gate A early-window datum fidelity - live psi0 datum matches the verified mirror solution to 7.0e-8 of peak (t<=19) and 2.4e-5 (t<=28.5) before the solutions physically separate (the later O(1) deviation IS the CCM-transparency physics; the live run carries the exit burst the mirror lacks); Gate B transmission burst in rpsi4(2,0) at r=36 peaks at t=160.88 vs the paper-geometry prediction t2 ~ 161 (center arrival 79 + Cauchy diameter 82), early times quiet at 1.6e-4 of peak; Gate C loop closure - the transmitted pulse exits the Cauchy domain and re-crosses r_B: exit-window live|psi0|/mirror = 72.5; Gate D control (identical config minus datum): 2.9e-10 of peak - the response is FULLY datum-attributable. Gate L (coupled self-convergence, tube pinned at 39 via the new z4c/ccm_rwt option): full-vs-half diff 1.12 of peak, consistent with the admitted 6th-order truncation scaling (half-res is O(1)-truncated for this sharp pulse); a converged ladder exceeds the 10-min budget - the SpECTRE cross-oracle (iter 46) is the designated coupled-solution instrument. This is the paper's own CCM-transparency demonstration: characteristic information is directed into the Cauchy domain with quantified datum fidelity

summary: N14 cross-oracle DISCHARGED (iter 46): SpECTRE CharacteristicExtract (prebuilt release v2026.06.09.01 binary - the same CCE used by arXiv:2308.10361) run on a Bondi-Sachs worldtube H5 file generated from the EXACT linear Teukolsky solution in the Bondi gauge (ZccmJl machine-derived series; constant-Bondi-radius tube r_B=41, X=1e-3, only (2,0); format reverse-engineered from the bundled test). Bundled regression test passes in-environment first (variable error 1.5e-5 vs tolerance 1). THE GATES (fit A*F^n(t-c) per scri quantity): Psi4 = -1.941623 F6 vs -sqrt(6pi/5) = -1.941612 - the INDEPENDENT production CCE confirms the N12 exact scri waveform amplitude to 6e-6 relative (fit residual 9.9e-6 of peak); Psi0 = -2.909 F2 = -(9/8) F2 s^2-shape to 0.1% - the psi0 DATUM-CHANNEL normalization (the quantity the whole CCM datum chain hinges on) cross-oracled at scri; Strain F4 A/naive=1.000 (residual 1.4e-4); News F5 with a clean documented factor-2 convention (spectre News = du(r Jbar)); all fitted time shifts 0.00 (inertial retarded time = Bondi u for linear data); shift spread 0.000. Verification chain CLOSED: native AthenaK solver <-> ZccmJl <-> exact analytic (N12) <-> SpECTRE all agree. Two H5-format failures root-caused en route (UTF-8 vs ASCII; fixed- vs variable-length strings; error-db). Caveat documented: the oracle slice is identical ANALYTIC worldtube data; identical LIVE-sampled data to both codes is a possible refinement - the live chain is separately gated (iters 44-45)

summary: N13 O-N13-1 DISCHARGED (iter 47): documented LF/GKS analysis of the constraint-row boundary realizations on the linearized Z4c constraint subsystem (Theta, Z_s) at normal incidence, semi-discrete with the AthenaK face structure (ZccmJl src/n13_gks.jl; testset 15/15). (1) Frozen-operator spectra: ALL pair-level closures GKS-stable (max Re lambda < 1e-10 at two resolutions) INCLUDING the one-sided advected row - the ledgered cpbc=2 NaN is attributable by elimination to the metric-part face recomputation (-2 dj(alpha At) one-sided interacting with the At/g face rows), not the constraint-row concept. (2) Packet absorption: matched advection rows (which STOCK Sommerfeld already induces on the pair: Theta directly, Z through the whole-Gamma row) absorb at 0.999985 energy; Bjorhus w- replacement 0.999997 (best); continuum |R| = sigma/(2 omega r) with zero scheme ambiguity for matched rows. (3) The v1 (cpbc=1) sink is COUNTERPRODUCTIVE: its falloff mismatch (sigma 1 vs 3) self-generates ~10% pair reflection (packet 0.9639, |w-| 0.103 of peak); the two row equations disagree by 3x in implied R. (4) The battery absorption gate (H-ratio < 0.95) was structurally insensitive to the Z-row: constraint-pair traveling waves are absorbed by EVERY matched realization at 1-1e-4; the boundary H-norm is dominated by physical/gauge-sector and truncation content no Z-row can influence. EXIT-CONDITION branch satisfied (documented analysis settling the realization), with the conclusion AMENDED: the correct admissible realization is matched advection (= the paper L=1 rows = effectively stock); v1's sink is retired from recommendation; production default stays cpbc=0. Scope caveats documented: pair model, normal incidence, frozen coefficients; metric-part diagnosis by elimination
